item.title: From the Hugging Face Hub to robot hardware with Strands Agents and LeRobot
item.description: Focus: Hugging Face has a new AI-related update around 'From the Hugging Face Hub to robot hardware with Strands Agents and LeRobot', relevant to product, industry or technical direction.

item.title: MolmoMotion: Language-guided 3D motion forecasting
item.description: Focus: Hugging Face Blog has a new AI-related update around 'MolmoMotion: Language-guided 3D motion forecasting', relevant to product, industry or technical direction.

item.title: A near-autonomous AI chemist improves a challenging reaction in medicinal chemistry
item.description: Focus: OpenAI and Molecule.one show how a near-autonomous AI chemist using GPT-5.4 improved a key drug-making reaction, advancing medicinal chemistry research.

item.title: GLM-5.2: Built for Long-Horizon Tasks
item.description: Focus: Hugging Face Blog has a new AI-related update around 'GLM-5.2: Built for Long-Horizon Tasks', relevant to product, industry or technical direction.

item.title: Introducing LifeSciBench
item.description: Focus: Introducing LifeSciBench, an expert-authored, expert-reviewed benchmark for evaluating how AI systems handle real-world life science research tasks and decisions.

item.title: Agentic Resource Discovery: Let agents search
item.description: Focus: Hugging Face Blog has a new AI-related update around 'Agentic Resource Discovery: Let agents search', relevant to product, industry or technical direction.

item.title: 🔬 The Self-Driving Lab — Joseph Krause, Radical AI
item.description: Focus: Radical AI's Joseph Krause on why the moat in materials is the lab, not the model

item.title: How to design AI agent loops: schedules, goals, and subagents in Claude Code and Codex
item.description: Focus: Lenny's Podcast is covered in a new interview or podcast. The core topic is 'How to design AI agent loops: schedules, goals, and subagents in Claude Code and Codex', useful for tracking executive views, AI strategy and product direction.

item.title: [AINews] GLM-5.2: the top Frontend Coding model in the world, IndexShare for Speculative Decoding
item.description: Focus: We have a new top open model in the world!

item.title: Kimi K2.7 Code vs Claude Fable 5: Landing pages that cost 94% less
item.description: Focus: We generated 12 landing pages with Kimi K2.7 Code and Claude Fable 5. Kimi cost 94% less and scored within a few points on every page. Here's what actually moved the needle.

item.title: Vercel Ship 2026 recap
item.description: Focus: For a decade, Vercel has shaped how the web is built. Now, we’re doing the same for agents. The companies that win the next decade will build on infrastructure designed for agents from the start, and over 2,500 people gathered in London this week to do just that at Vercel Ship 2026. Agentic i

item.title: The Agent Stack
item.description: Focus: Agents are designed to do almost any kind of work, from answering support tickets to writing code. No matter how complex the workload, how long it runs, or how many turns it takes to complete, every agent needs three core capabilities to operate: Agents need to connect to models and route bet

item.title: Introducing Vercel Connect
item.description: Focus: Giving your agents access to your tools, data, and services is what makes them useful. As agents perform deeper work across systems, authenticating and authorizing that access becomes central to your application architecture. Today, agent access is usually granted through long-lived provider

item.title: Introducing eve
item.description: Focus: Today, we are proud to introduce eve , an open-source agent framework for building, running, and scaling agents. eve is designed around the idea that building an agent should mean defining what it does without assembling all of the pieces that it needs to run in production. Instead, eve comes

item.title: CLI deployment limits removed
item.description: Focus: We've removed CLI-specific deployment limits, making it easier to deploy from local machine and external CI/CD pipelines with instant feedback. Teams and AI agents can now deploy at the pace their workflows demand. Learn more about limits in the Documentation .

item.title: Vercel Passport is now in Public Beta
item.description: Focus: Enterprise teams can now control access to their Vercel deployments with Vercel Passport , using their own identity provider. Visitors authenticate through providers like Okta, Auth0, or any compatible OIDC provider before they can view a protected deployment. Use Passport to: Reuse an OIDC a

item.title: Vercel Connect: Secure access to external services for your agents
item.description: Focus: Vercel Connect lets your apps and agents access external services like Slack, GitHub, Salesforce, and your own custom APIs without storing a long-lived provider secret in your environment. You register a connector once, and your code requests scoped, short-lived tokens at runtime, only when i

item.title: Introducing eve, an open-source agent framework
item.description: Focus: eve is now available in public preview. eve is an open-source framework for building, running, and scaling agents. An agent is just a directory of files, and production comes built in: Durable execution Sandboxed compute Human-in-the-loop approvals Subagents Evals The smallest agent that runs
